name: Commission breakdown redesign
motivation: Credited placements section currently shows only net payable amount, making it impossible for producers to understand: (1) what they notionally earned (gross commission), (2) what is held and why, (3) the gap between gross and net. This undermines trust and creates support burden.
intended_experience: Producers see a detailed commission breakdown card for each placement showing: notional gross commission → deductions/holds (collection gate, guarantee window, draw offset, split adjustments) → net payable. Visual breakdown with reason tags helps producers understand hold mechanics. Demo data provides realistic mix of scenarios (collected, tiered rates, held, guaranteed, split, retained-phase).
constraints: Must preserve existing CommissionRecord API schema (gross_commission, net_payable, hold_reason, status already available)
Frontend-only change to CreditedPlacements view; no API contract changes
Seed data must be consolidated into demo-placement-seam.ts so all demo scenarios run together without breaking E2E tests
Demo producer account must show all 6+ realistic commission states in a single login
Redesign must work with existing calculation-engine.ts (no changes to hold logic)

frontend: apps/web/src/components/portal/ProducerPortal.tsx - may need layout adjustments for taller cards
tests: tests/e2e/producer-portal.test.ts - verify demo data scenarios render correctly

public_contracts: No API changes; existing GET /me/commission-records endpoint already returns all needed fields
shared_state_changes: Demo seed data will consolidate into single EXTRA_DEMO_PLACEMENTS array to avoid duplication
test_surfaces: UI breakdown rendering: gross, holds, net payable alignment
Hold reason tags display (collection_gate, guarantee_hold, held_pending_phase_invoice)
Demo scenarios: collected placement shows full net payable; held placement shows $0 net with reason; split shows reduced gross; guaranteed shows hold reason + expiry
Status transitions: verify Held/Payable/Paid status chips align with hold state
